tEn: Mastering Diverse Domains through World Models
tZh: 通过世界模型掌握多样领域
sEn: DreamerV3 is a general algorithm that outperforms specialized methods across over 150 diverse tasks with a single configuration, learning a world model to imagine future scenarios.
sZh: DreamerV3是一种通用算法，通过单一配置在超过150个不同任务上超越专门方法，它学习环境模型并想象未来场景来改进行为。
absEn: Developing a general algorithm that learns to solve tasks across a wide range of applications has been a fundamental challenge in artificial intelligence. Although current reinforcement learning algorithms can be readily applied to tasks similar to what they have been developed for, configuring them for new application domains requires significant human expertise and experimentation. We present DreamerV3, a general algorithm that outperforms specialized methods across over 150 diverse tasks, with a single configuration. Dreamer learns a model of the environment and improves its behavior by imagining future scenarios. Robustness techniques based on normalization, balancing, and transformations enable stable learning across domains. Applied out of the box, Dreamer is the first algorithm to collect diamonds in Minecraft from scratch without human data or curricula. This achievement has been posed as a significant challenge in artificial intelligence that requires exploring farsighted strategies from pixels and sparse rewards in an open world. Our work allows solving challenging control problems without extensive experimentation, making reinforcement learning broadly applicable.
absZh: 开发一种能够学习解决广泛领域任务的通用算法一直是人工智能的基本挑战。尽管当前的强化学习算法可以轻松应用于与其开发环境相似的任务，但将它们配置到新的应用领域需要大量的人类专业知识和实验。我们提出了DreamerV3，这是一种通用算法，通过单一配置在超过150个不同任务上超越了专门方法。Dreamer学习环境模型，并通过想象未来场景来改进其行为。基于归一化、平衡和变换的鲁棒性技术使其能够在不同领域稳定学习。开箱即用，Dreamer是第一个从零开始在Minecraft中收集钻石的算法，无需人类数据或课程。这一成就被认为是人工智能中的重大挑战，需要从像素和稀疏奖励中探索远见策略。我们的工作使得无需大量实验即可解决具有挑战性的控制问题，从而使得强化学习具有广泛的适用性。

contrib.en: Proposes DreamerV3, a general algorithm mastering diverse domains with fixed hyperparameters.
contrib.zh: 提出 DreamerV3，一种使用固定超参数掌握多种领域的通用算法。

contrib.en: Demonstrates favorable scaling properties where larger models improve performance and data-efficiency.
contrib.zh: 展示了有利的缩放特性，更大的模型可提升性能和样本效率。

contrib.en: Outperforms specialized algorithms across over 150 tasks in 7 benchmarks.
contrib.zh: 在 7 个基准测试的 150 多个任务上超越专用算法。

contrib.en: First algorithm to collect diamonds in Minecraft from scratch without human data or curricula.
contrib.zh: 首个无需人类数据或课程即可在 Minecraft 中从头收集钻石的算法。

contrib.en: Introduces symlog predictions and robust normalization for stable learning across domains.
contrib.zh: 引入 symlog 预测和鲁棒归一化技术，实现跨领域的稳定学习。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art on continuous control, BSuite, and Crafter benchmarks.
contrib.zh: 在连续控制、BSuite 和 Crafter 基准测试上达到最先进水平。

limits.en: DreamerV3 only learns to sometimes collect diamonds in Minecraft within 100M steps.
limits.zh: DreamerV3 仅在 1 亿步内偶尔学会在 Minecraft 中收集钻石。

limits.en: Increases block breaking speed for Minecraft learning, which may not generalize.
limits.zh: 为学习 Minecraft 提高了方块破坏速度，可能无法泛化。

limits.en: Scaling properties need further validation at larger model sizes.
limits.zh: 缩放特性需要在更大模型规模下进一步验证。

limits.en: Trains separate agents per task, lacking transfer learning across tasks.
limits.zh: 每个任务训练独立智能体，缺乏跨任务迁移学习。

limits.en: Requires significant compute resources (one V100 GPU per agent).
limits.zh: 需要大量算力资源（每个智能体一块 V100 GPU）。

items.en: L[1]Q[l,#1] \NewColumnTypeC[1]Q[c,#1] \NewColumnTypeR[1]Q[r,#1] \NewEnvironmytabular[1] \BODY
items.zh: L[1]Q[l,#1] \NewColumnTypeC[1]Q[c,#1] \NewColumnTypeR[1]Q[r,#1] \NewEnvironmytabular[1] \BODY

items.en: General intelligence requires solving tasks across many domains. Current reinforcement learning algorithms carry this potential but are held back by the resources and knowledge required to tune them for new tasks. We present DreamerV3, a general and scalable algorithm based on world models that outperforms previous approaches across a wide range of domains with fixed hyperparameters. These domains include continuous and discrete actions, visual and low-dimensional inputs, 2D and 3D worlds, different data budgets, reward frequencies, and reward scales. We observe favorable scaling properties of DreamerV3, with larger models directly translating to higher data-efficiency and final performance. Applied out of the box, DreamerV3 is the first algorithm to collect diamonds in Minecraft from scratch without human data or curricula, a long-standing challenge in artificial intelligence. Our general algorithm makes reinforcement learning broadly applicable and allows scaling to hard decision making problems.
items.zh: 通用智能需要解决跨多个领域的任务。当前的强化学习算法具有这种潜力，但受限于为适应新任务而调整它们所需的资源和知识。我们提出了DreamerV3，一种基于世界模型的通用且可扩展的算法，在固定超参数的情况下，在广泛领域内优于先前的方法。这些领域包括连续和离散动作、视觉和低维输入、2D和3D世界、不同的数据预算、奖励频率和奖励尺度。我们观察到DreamerV3具有良好的缩放特性，更大的模型直接转化为更高的数据效率和最终性能。开箱即用，DreamerV3是第一个从零开始收集Minecraft钻石的算法，无需人类数据或课程，这是人工智能领域长期存在的挑战。我们的通用算法使强化学习具有广泛的适用性，并允许扩展到困难的决策问题。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Using the same hyperparameters across all domains, DreamerV3 outperforms specialized model-free and model-based algorithms in a wide range of benchmarks and data-efficiency regimes. Applied out of the box, DreamerV3 also learns to obtain diamonds in the popular video game Minecraft from scratch given sparse rewards, a long-standing challenge in artificial intelligence for which previous approaches required human data or domain-specific heuristics.
items.capZh: 图1：在所有领域使用相同的超参数，DreamerV3在广泛的基准测试和数据效率机制中优于专门的无模型和基于模型的算法。开箱即用，DreamerV3还能在稀疏奖励的情况下从零开始学习在热门视频游戏Minecraft中获得钻石，这是人工智能领域长期存在的挑战，此前的方法需要人类数据或领域特定的启发式方法。

items.en: Reinforcement learning has enabled computers to solve individual tasks through interaction, such as surpassing humans in the games of Go and Dota 1, 2. However, applying algorithms to new application domains, for example from board games to video games or robotics tasks, requires expert knowledge and computational resources for tuning the algorithms 3. This brittleness also hinders scaling to large models that are expensive to tune. Different domains pose unique learning challenges that have prompted specialized algorithms, such as for continuous control 4, 5, sparse rewards 6, 7, image inputs 8, 9, and spatial environments 10, 11. Creating a general algorithm that learns to master new domains out of the box—without tuning—would overcome the barrier of expert knowledge and open up reinforcement learning to a wide range of practical applications.
items.zh: 强化学习使计算机能够通过交互解决单个任务，例如在围棋和Dota游戏中超越人类1, 2。然而，将算法应用于新的应用领域，例如从棋盘游戏到视频游戏或机器人任务，需要专家知识和计算资源来调整算法3。这种脆弱性也阻碍了扩展到难以调整的大型模型。不同领域提出了独特的学习挑战，促使了专门的算法，例如用于连续控制4, 5、稀疏奖励6, 7、图像输入8, 9和空间环境10, 11。创建一个能够开箱即用（无需调整）掌握新领域的通用算法，将克服专家知识的障碍，并为强化学习开辟广泛的实际应用。

items.en: We present DreamerV3, a general and scalable algorithm that masters a wide range of domains with fixed hyperparameters, outperforming specialized algorithms. DreamerV3 learns a world model 12, 13, 14 from experience for rich perception and imagination training. The algorithm consists of 3 neural networks: the world model predicts future outcomes of potential actions, the critic judges the value of each situation, and the actor learns to reach valuable situations. We enable learning across domains with fixed hyperparameters by transforming signal magnitudes and through robust normalization techniques. To provide practical guidelines for solving new challenges, we investigating the scaling behavior of DreamerV3. Notably, we demonstrate that increasing the model size of DreamerV3 monotonically improves both its final performance and data-efficiency.
items.zh: 我们提出了DreamerV3，一种通用且可扩展的算法，在固定超参数的情况下掌握广泛领域，优于专门的算法。DreamerV3从经验中学习世界模型12, 13, 14，用于丰富的感知和想象训练。该算法由3个神经网络组成：世界模型预测潜在动作的未来结果，评论者判断每种情况的价值，行动者学习达到有价值的情况。我们通过变换信号幅度和鲁棒归一化技术，实现了在固定超参数下跨领域的学习。为了提供解决新挑战的实用指南，我们研究了DreamerV3的缩放行为。值得注意的是，我们证明增加DreamerV3的模型大小单调地提高了其最终性能和数据效率。

items.en: The popular video game Minecraft has become a focal point of reinforcement learning research in recent years, with international competitions held for learning to collect diamonds in Minecraft 15. Solving this challenge without human data has been widely recognized as a milestone for artificial intelligence because of the sparse rewards, exploration difficulty, and long time horizons in this procedurally generated open-world environment. Due to these obstacles, previous approaches resorted to human expert data and manually-crafted curricula 16, 17. DreamerV3 is the first algorithm to collect diamonds in Minecraft from scratch, solving this challenge.
items.zh: 近年来，热门视频游戏Minecraft已成为强化学习研究的焦点，国际竞赛旨在学习在Minecraft中收集钻石15。由于稀疏奖励、探索困难以及这个程序生成的开放世界环境中的长时间跨度，在没有人类数据的情况下解决这一挑战被广泛认为是人工智能的一个里程碑。由于这些障碍，以前的方法依赖于人类专家数据和手工制作的课程16, 17。DreamerV3是第一个从零开始收集Minecraft钻石的算法，解决了这一挑战。

items.en: We summarize the four key contributions of this paper as follows:
items.zh: 我们总结了本文的四个关键贡献如下：

items.en: We present DreamerV3, a general algorithm that learns to master diverse domains while using fixed hyperparameters, making reinforcement learning readily applicable.
items.zh: 我们提出了DreamerV3，一种通用算法，能够在使用固定超参数的情况下学习掌握多样化的领域，使强化学习易于应用。

items.en: We demonstrate the favorable scaling properties of DreamerV3, where increased model size leads to monotonic improvements in final performance and data-efficiency.
items.zh: 我们展示了DreamerV3良好的缩放特性，其中模型大小的增加导致最终性能和数据效率的单调提升。

items.en: We perform an extensive evaluation, showing that DreamerV3 outperforms more specialized algorithms across domains, and release the training curves of all methods to facilitate comparison.
items.zh: 我们进行了广泛的评估，表明DreamerV3在多个领域优于更专门的算法，并发布了所有方法的训练曲线以方便比较。

items.en: We find that DreamerV3 is the first algorithm to collect diamonds in Minecraft from scratch without human data or curricula, solving a long-standing challenge in artificial intelligence.
items.zh: 我们发现DreamerV3是第一个从零开始收集Minecraft钻石的算法，无需人类数据或课程，解决了人工智能领域长期存在的挑战。

items.en: The DreamerV3 algorithm consists of 3 neural networks—the world model, the critic, and the actor—that are trained concurrently from replayed experience without sharing gradients, as shown in Figure 3. To succeed across domains, these components need to accommodate different signal magnitudes and robustly balance terms in their objectives. This is challenging as we are not only targeting similar tasks within the same domain but aim to learn across different domains with fixed hyperparameters. This section first explains a simple transformation for predicting quantities of unknown orders of magnitude. We then introduce the world model, critic, and actor and their robust learning objectives. Specifically, we find that combining KL balancing and free bits enables the world model to learn without tuning, and scaling down large returns without amplifying small returns allows a fixed policy entropy regularizer. The differences to DreamerV2 are detailed in Appendix C.
items.zh: DreamerV3算法由3个神经网络组成——世界模型、评论者和行动者——它们从重放的经验中同时训练，不共享梯度，如图3所示。为了在多个领域取得成功，这些组件需要适应不同的信号幅度，并鲁棒地平衡其目标中的各项。这具有挑战性，因为我们不仅针对同一领域内的类似任务，而且旨在使用固定超参数跨不同领域学习。本节首先解释了一种简单的变换，用于预测未知数量级的目标。然后我们介绍了世界模型、评论者和行动者及其鲁棒的学习目标。具体来说，我们发现结合KL平衡和自由比特使世界模型无需调整即可学习，而缩小大回报而不放大微小回报允许固定的策略熵正则化器。与DreamerV2的差异详见附录C。

items.en: Reconstructing inputs and predicting rewards and values can be challenging because their scale can vary across domains. Predicting large targets using a squared loss can lead to divergence whereas absolute and Huber losses 18 stagnate learning. On the other hand, normalizing targets based on running statistics 19 introduces non-stationarity into the optimization. We suggest symlog predictions as a simple solution to this dilemma. For this, a neural network f​(x,θ)𝑓𝑥𝜃f(x,\theta) with inputs x𝑥x and parameters θ𝜃\theta learns to predict a transformed version of its targets y𝑦y. To read out predictions y^^𝑦\hat{y} of the network, we apply the inverse transformation:
items.zh: 重构输入和预测奖励及值可能具有挑战性，因为它们的尺度可能因领域而异。使用平方损失预测大目标可能导致发散，而绝对损失和Huber损失18会停滞学习。另一方面，基于运行统计量19归一化目标会引入非平稳性到优化中。我们建议使用symlog预测作为这一困境的简单解决方案。为此，神经网络f(x,θ)（输入x，参数θ）学习预测其目标y的变换版本。为了读出网络的预测ŷ，我们应用逆变换：

items.en: As shown in Figure 4, using the logarithm as transformation would not allow us to predict targets that take on negative values. Therefore, we choose a function from the bi-symmetric logarithmic family 20 that we name symlog as the transformation with the symexp function as its inverse:
items.zh: 如图4所示，使用对数作为变换不允许我们预测取负值的目标。因此，我们从双对称对数族20中选择一个函数，我们称之为symlog作为变换，其逆函数为symexp：

items.capEn: Figure 4: The symlog function compared to logarithm and identity.
items.capZh: 图4：symlog函数与对数和恒等函数的比较。

items.en: The symlog function compresses the magnitudes of both large positive and negative values. Unlike the logarithm, it is symmetric around the origin while preserving the input sign. This allows the optimization process to quickly move the network predictions to large values when needed. Symlog approximates the identity around the origin so that it does not affect learning of targets that are already small enough. For critic learning, a more involved transformation has previously been proposed 21, which we found less effective on average across domains.
items.zh: symlog函数压缩了大正数和大负数的幅度。与对数不同，它在原点附近对称，同时保留输入的符号。这使得优化过程能够在需要时快速将网络预测推向大值。Symlog在原点附近近似于恒等函数，因此不会影响对已经足够小的目标的学习。对于评论家学习，先前已有更复杂的变换被提出21，但我们发现它在各领域平均效果较差。

items.en: DreamerV3 uses symlog predictions in the decoder, the reward predictor, and the critic. It also squashes inputs to the encoder using the symlog function. Despite its simplicity, this approach robustly and quickly learns across a diverse range of environments. With symlog predictions, there is no need for truncating large rewards 18, introducing non-stationary through reward normalization 19, or adjusting network weights when new extreme values are detected 22.
items.zh: DreamerV3在解码器、奖励预测器和评论家中使用symlog预测。它还使用symlog函数对编码器的输入进行压缩。尽管简单，但该方法能在多种环境中稳健且快速地学习。使用symlog预测，无需截断大奖励18、通过奖励归一化引入非平稳性19，或在检测到新的极值时调整网络权重22。

items.en: The world model learns compact representations of sensory inputs through autoencoding 23, 24 and enables planning by predicting future representations and rewards for potential actions. We implement the world model as a Recurrent State-Space Model (RSSM) 14, as shown in Figure 3. First, an encoder maps sensory inputs xtsubscript𝑥𝑡x_{t} to stochastic representations ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t}. Then, a sequence model with recurrent state htsubscriptℎ𝑡h_{t} predicts the sequence of these representations given past actions at−1subscript𝑎𝑡1a_{t-1}. The concatenation of htsubscriptℎ𝑡h_{t} and ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t} forms the model state from which we predict rewards rtsubscript𝑟𝑡r_{t} and episode continuation flags ct∈{0,1}subscript𝑐𝑡01c_{t}\in\{0,1\} and reconstruct the inputs to ensure informative representations:
items.zh: 世界模型通过自编码23, 24学习感官输入的紧凑表示，并通过预测潜在动作的未来表示和奖励来实现规划。我们将世界模型实现为循环状态空间模型（RSSM）14，如图3所示。首先，编码器将感官输入x_t映射为随机表示z_t。然后，具有循环状态h_t的序列模型根据过去动作a_{t-1}预测这些表示的序列。h_t和z_t的拼接构成模型状态，我们从中预测奖励r_t和回合继续标志c_t ∈ {0,1}，并重构输入以确保表示的信息性：

items.tex:
\displaystyle\begin{aligned} \begin{aligned} \raisebox{8.39578pt}{\hbox to0.0pt{\hss\vbox to0.0pt{\hbox{$\text{RSSM}\hskip 4.30554pt\begin{cases}\hphantom{A}\\
\hphantom{A}\\
\hphantom{A}\end{cases}$}\vss}}}&\text{Sequence model:}\hskip 35.00005pt&&h_{t}&\ =&\ f_{\phi}(h_{t-1},z_{t-1},a_{t-1})\\
&\text{Encoder:}\hskip 35.00005pt&&z_{t}&\ \sim&\ q_{\phi}(z_{t}\;|\;h_{t},x_{t})\\
&\text{Dynamics predictor:}\hskip 35.00005pt&&\hat{z}_{t}&\ \sim&\ p_{\phi}(\hat{z}_{t}\;|\;h_{t})\\
&\text{Reward predictor:}\hskip 35.00005pt&&\hat{r}_{t}&\ \sim&\ p_{\phi}(\hat{r}_{t}\;|\;h_{t},z_{t})\\
&\text{Continue predictor:}\hskip 35.00005pt&&\hat{c}_{t}&\ \sim&\ p_{\phi}(\hat{c}_{t}\;|\;h_{t},z_{t})\\
&\text{Decoder:}\hskip 35.00005pt&&\hat{x}_{t}&\ \sim&\ p_{\phi}(\hat{x}_{t}\;|\;h_{t},z_{t})\end{aligned}\end{aligned}

items.capEn: Figure 5: Multi-step video predictions in DMLab (top) and Control Suite (bottom). From 5 frames of context input, the model predicts 45 steps into the future given the action sequence and without access to intermediate images. The world model learns an understanding of the underlying 3D structure of the two environments. Refer to Appendix H for additional video predictions.
items.capZh: 图5：DMLab（上）和Control Suite（下）中的多步视频预测。从5帧上下文输入开始，模型根据动作序列预测未来45步，且无法访问中间图像。世界模型学习了对两个环境底层3D结构的理解。更多视频预测请参见附录H。

items.en: The prediction loss trains the decoder and reward predictor via the symlog loss and the continue predictor via binary classification loss. The dynamics loss trains the sequence model to predict the next representation by minimizing the KL divergence between the predictor pϕ​(zt|ht)subscript𝑝italic-ϕconditionalsubscript𝑧𝑡subscriptℎ𝑡p_{\phi}(z_{t}\;|\;h_{t}) and the next stochastic representation qϕ​(zt|ht,xt)subscript𝑞italic-ϕconditionalsubscript𝑧𝑡subscriptℎ𝑡subscript𝑥𝑡q_{\phi}(z_{t}\;|\;h_{t},x_{t}). The representation loss trains the representations to become more predictable if the dynamics cannot predict their distribution, allowing us to use a factorized dynamics predictor for fast sampling when training the actor critic. The two losses differ in the stop-gradient operator sg⁡(⋅)sg⋅\operatorname{sg}(\cdot) and their loss scale. To avoid a degenerate solution where the dynamics are trivial to predict but contain not enough information about the inputs, we employ free bits28 by clipping the dynamics and representation losses below the value of 1 nat ≈\approx 1.44 bits. This disables them while they are already minimized well to focus the world model on its prediction loss:
items.zh: 预测损失通过symlog损失训练解码器和奖励预测器，并通过二分类损失训练继续预测器。动力学损失通过最小化预测器p_φ(z_t | h_t)与下一个随机表示q_φ(z_t | h_t, x_t)之间的KL散度，训练序列模型预测下一个表示。表示损失训练表示在动力学无法预测其分布时变得更可预测，从而允许在训练演员-评论家时使用因子化动力学预测器进行快速采样。这两个损失在停止梯度算子sg(·)和损失尺度上有所不同。为避免动力学易于预测但包含的输入信息不足的退化解，我们采用自由位28，将动力学和表示损失裁剪到1 nat ≈ 1.44 bits以下。当它们已被充分最小化时，这会使它们失效，从而使世界模型专注于预测损失：

items.en: Previous world models require scaling the representation loss differently based on the visual complexity of the environment. Complex 3D environments contain details unnecessary for control and thus prompt a stronger regularizer to simplify the representations and make them more predictable. In 2D games, the background is often static and individual pixels may matter for the task, requiring a weak regularizer to perceive fine details. We find that combining free bits with a small scale for the representation loss resolve this dilemma, allowing for fixed hyperparameters across domains. Moreover, symlog predictions for the decoder unify the gradient scale of the prediction loss across environments, further stabilizing the trade-off with the representation loss.
items.zh: 先前的世界模型需要根据环境的视觉复杂度不同来缩放表示损失。复杂的3D环境包含控制不需要的细节，因此需要更强的正则化器来简化表示并使其更可预测。在2D游戏中，背景通常是静态的，单个像素可能对任务重要，因此需要弱正则化器来感知精细细节。我们发现将自由位与较小的表示损失尺度相结合解决了这一困境，允许跨领域使用固定超参数。此外，解码器的symlog预测统一了跨环境的预测损失梯度尺度，进一步稳定了与表示损失的权衡。

items.en: We occasionally observed spikes the in KL losses in earlier experiments, consistent with reports for deep variational autoencoders 29, 30. To prevent this, we parameterize the categorical distributions of the encoder and dynamics predictor as mixtures of 1% uniform and 99% neural network output, making it impossible for them to become near deterministic and thus ensuring well-scaled KL losses. Further model details and hyperparameters are summarized in Appendix W.
items.zh: 我们偶尔在早期实验中观察到KL损失中出现尖峰，这与深度变分自编码器的报告一致29, 30。为防止这种情况，我们将编码器和动力学预测器的分类分布参数化为1%均匀分布和99%神经网络输出的混合，使其不可能变得接近确定性，从而确保良好缩放的KL损失。更多模型细节和超参数总结在附录W中。

items.en: The actor and critic neural networks learn behaviors purely from abstract sequences predicted by the world model 12, 13. During environment interaction, we select actions by sampling from the actor network without lookahead planning. The actor and critic operate on model states st≐{ht,zt}approaches-limitsubscript𝑠𝑡subscriptℎ𝑡subscript𝑧𝑡s_{t}\doteq\{h_{t},z_{t}\} and thus benefit from the Markovian representations learned by the world model. The actor aims to maximize the expected return Rt≐∑τ=0∞γτ​rt+τapproaches-limitsubscript𝑅𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜏0superscript𝛾𝜏subscript𝑟𝑡𝜏R_{t}\doteq\textstyle\sum_{\tau=0}^{\infty}\gamma^{\tau}r_{t+\tau} with a discount factor γ=0.997𝛾0.997\gamma=0.997 for each model state. To consider rewards beyond the prediction horizon T=16𝑇16T=16, the critic learns to predict the return of each state under the current actor behavior:
items.zh: 演员和评论家神经网络仅从世界模型预测的抽象序列中学习行为12, 13。在与环境交互期间，我们通过从演员网络采样来选择动作，无需前瞻规划。演员和评论家对模型状态s_t ≐ {h_t, z_t}进行操作，因此受益于世界模型学习的马尔可夫表示。演员旨在最大化期望回报R_t ≐ ∑_{τ=0}^∞ γ^τ r_{t+τ}，折扣因子γ=0.997，针对每个模型状态。为了考虑预测范围T=16之外的奖励，评论家学习在当前演员行为下预测每个状态的回报：

items.en: Starting from representations of replayed inputs, the dynamics predictor and actor produce a sequence of imagined model states s1:Tsubscript𝑠:1𝑇s_{1:T}, actions a1:Tsubscript𝑎:1𝑇a_{1:T}, rewards r1:Tsubscript𝑟:1𝑇r_{1:T}, and continuation flags c1:Tsubscript𝑐:1𝑇c_{1:T}. To estimate returns that consider rewards beyond the prediction horizon, we compute bootstrapped λ𝜆\lambda-returns that integrate the predicted rewards and values 31, 32:
items.zh: 从重放输入的表示开始，动力学预测器和演员生成一系列想象模型状态s_{1:T}、动作a_{1:T}、奖励r_{1:T}和继续标志c_{1:T}。为了估计考虑预测范围之外奖励的回报，我们计算自举λ-回报，它整合了预测的奖励和值31, 32：

items.en: A simple choice for the critic loss function would be to regress the λ𝜆\lambda-returns via squared error or symlog predictions. However, the critic predicts the expected value of a potentially widespread return distribution, which can slow down learning. We choose a discrete regression approach for learning the critic based on twohot encoded targets 33, 34, 35, 36 that let the critic maintain and refine a distribution over potential returns. For this, we transform returns using the symlog function and discretize the resulting range into a sequence B𝐵B of K=255𝐾255K=255 equally spaced buckets bisubscript𝑏𝑖b_{i}. The critic network outputs a softmax distribution pψ​(bi|st)subscript𝑝𝜓conditionalsubscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑠𝑡p_{\psi}(b_{i}\;|\;s_{t}) over the buckets and its output is formed as the expected bucket value under this distribution. Importantly, the critic can predict any continuous value in the interval because its expected bucket value can fall between the buckets:
items.zh: 评论家损失函数的一个简单选择是通过平方误差或symlog预测对λ-回报进行回归。然而，评论家预测的是可能广泛分布的回报分布的期望值，这可能会减慢学习速度。我们选择一种离散回归方法来学习评论家，基于twohot编码的目标33, 34, 35, 36，让评论家维护并细化潜在回报的分布。为此，我们使用symlog函数变换回报，并将结果范围离散化为K=255个等间距桶b_i的序列B。评论家网络输出桶上的softmax分布p_ψ(b_i | s_t)，其输出为该分布下的期望桶值。重要的是，评论家可以预测区间内的任何连续值，因为其期望桶值可以落在桶之间：

items.en: To train the critic, we symlog transform the targets Rtλsubscriptsuperscript𝑅𝜆𝑡R^{\lambda}_{t} and then twohot encode them into a soft label for the softmax distribution produced by the critic. Twohot encoding is a generalization of onehot encoding to continuous values. It produces a vector of length |B|𝐵|B| where all elements are 00 except for the two entries closest to the encoded continuous number, at positions k𝑘k and k+1𝑘1k+1. These two entries sum up to 111, with more weight given to the entry that is closer to the encoded number:
items.zh: 为了训练评论家，我们对目标R_t^λ进行symlog变换，然后将其twohot编码为评论家产生的softmax分布的软标签。Twohot编码是onehot编码对连续值的推广。它产生一个长度为|B|的向量，其中所有元素均为0，除了最接近编码连续数的两个条目，位于位置k和k+1。这两个条目之和为1，权重更分配给更接近编码数的条目：

items.en: Given twohot encoded targets yt=sg⁡(twohot⁡(symlog⁡(Rtλ)))subscript𝑦𝑡sgtwohotsymlogsubscriptsuperscript𝑅𝜆𝑡y_{t}=\operatorname{sg}(\operatorname{twohot}(\operatorname{symlog}(R^{\lambda}_{t}))), where sg⁡(⋅)sg⋅\operatorname{sg}(\cdot) stops the gradient, the critic minimizes the categorical cross entropy loss for classification with soft targets:
items.zh: 给定twohot编码的目标y_t = sg(twohot(symlog(R_t^λ)))，其中sg(·)停止梯度，评论家最小化软目标分类的交叉熵损失：

items.en: We found discrete regression for the critic to accelerate learning especially in environments with sparse rewards, likely because of their bimodal reward and return distributions. We use the same discrete regression approach for the reward predictor of the world model.
items.zh: 我们发现评论家的离散回归加速了学习，尤其是在稀疏奖励环境中，可能是因为其双峰奖励和回报分布。我们对世界模型的奖励预测器使用相同的离散回归方法。

items.en: Because the critic regresses targets that depend on its own predictions, we stabilize learning by regularizing the critic towards predicting the outputs of an exponentially moving average of its own parameters. This is similar to target networks used previously in reinforcement learning 18 but allows us to compute returns using the current critic network. We further noticed that the randomly initialized reward predictor and critic networks at the start of training can result in large predicted rewards that can delay the onset of learning. We initialize the output weights of the reward predictor and critic to zeros, which effectively alleviates the problem and accelerates early learning.
items.zh: 由于评论家回归的目标依赖于其自身的预测，我们通过正则化评论家使其预测自身参数的指数移动平均输出来稳定学习。这类似于先前在强化学习中使用的目标网络18，但允许我们使用当前评论家网络计算回报。我们还注意到，训练开始时随机初始化的奖励预测器和评论家网络可能导致大的预测奖励，从而延迟学习的开始。我们将奖励预测器和评论家的输出权重初始化为零，这有效缓解了问题并加速了早期学习。

items.en: The actor network learns to choose actions that maximize returns while ensuring sufficient exploration through an entropy regularizer 37. However, the scale of this regularizer heavily depends on the scale and frequency of rewards in the environment, which has been a challenge for previous algorithms 38. Ideally, we would like the policy to explore quickly in the absence of nearby returns without sacrificing final performance under dense returns.
items.zh: 演员网络学习选择最大化回报的动作，同时通过熵正则化器确保充分探索37。然而，该正则化器的尺度严重依赖于环境中的奖励尺度和频率，这曾是先前算法的挑战38。理想情况下，我们希望策略在没有附近回报时快速探索，而在密集回报下不牺牲最终性能。

items.en: To stabilize the scale of returns, we normalize them using moving statistics. For tasks with dense rewards, one can simply divide returns by their standard deviation, similar to previous work 19. However, when rewards are sparse, the return standard deviation is generally small and this approach would amplify the noise contained in near-zero returns, resulting in an overly deterministic policy that fails to explore. Therefore, we propose propose to scale down large returns without scaling up small returns. We implement this idea by dividing returns by their scale S𝑆S, for which we discuss multiple choices below, but only if they exceed a minimum threshold of 111. This simple change is the key to allowing a single entropy scale η=3⋅10−4𝜂⋅3superscript104\eta=3\cdot 10^{-4} across dense and sparse rewards:
items.zh: 为了稳定回报的尺度，我们使用移动统计量对其进行归一化。对于密集奖励任务，可以简单地将回报除以其标准差，类似于先前工作19。然而，当奖励稀疏时，回报标准差通常很小，这种方法会放大接近零回报中的噪声，导致策略过于确定性而无法探索。因此，我们提出缩小大回报而不放大小的回报。我们通过将回报除以其尺度S来实现这一想法，下面讨论多种选择，但仅当尺度超过最小阈值1时才进行。这一简单改变是允许单一熵尺度η=3·10^{-4}跨密集和稀疏奖励的关键：

items.en: We follow DreamerV2 27 in estimating the gradient of the first term by stochastic backpropagation for continuous actions and by reinforce 39 for discrete actions. The gradient of the second term is computed in closed form.
items.zh: 我们遵循DreamerV2 27，通过随机反向传播估计连续动作的第一项梯度，通过REINFORCE 39估计离散动作的梯度。第二项的梯度以封闭形式计算。

items.en: In deterministic environments, we find that normalizing returns by their exponentially decaying standard deviation suffices. However, for heavily randomized environments, the return distribution can be highly non-Gaussian and contain outliers of large returns caused by a small number of particularly easy episodes, leading to an overly deterministic policy that struggles to sufficiently explore. To normalize returns while being robust to such outliers, we scale returns by an exponentially decaying average of the range from their 5th to their 95th batch percentile:
items.zh: 在确定性环境中，我们发现通过指数衰减标准差归一化回报就足够了。然而，对于高度随机化的环境，回报分布可能高度非高斯，并包含由少数特别容易的回合导致的大回报异常值，导致策略过于确定性而难以充分探索。为了在归一化回报时对异常值具有鲁棒性，我们通过从第5到第95批百分位范围的指数衰减平均值来缩放回报：

items.en: Because a constant return offset does not affect the objective, this is equivalent to an affine transformation that maps these percentiles to 00 and 111, respectively. Compared to advantage normalization 19, scaling returns down accelerates exploration under sparse rewards without sacrificing final performance under dense rewards, while using a fixed entropy scale.
items.zh: 由于恒定的回报偏移不影响目标，这等价于将这些百分位分别映射到0和1的仿射变换。与优势归一化19相比，缩小回报在稀疏奖励下加速探索，而在密集奖励下不牺牲最终性能，同时使用固定的熵尺度。

items.en: We perform an extensive empirical study to evaluate the generality and scalability of DreamerV3 across diverse domains—with over 150 tasks—under fixed hyperparameters. We designed the experiments to compare DreamerV3 to the best methods in the literature, which are often specifically designed to the benchmark at hand. Moreover, we apply DreamerV3 to the challenging video game Minecraft. Appendix A gives an overview of the domains. For DreamerV3, we directly report the performance of the stochastic training policy and avoid separate evaluation runs using the deterministic policy, simplifying the setup. All DreamerV3 agents are trained on one Nvidia V100 GPU each, making the algorithm widely usable across research labs. The source code and numerical results are available on the project website: https://danijar.com/dreamerv3
items.zh: 我们进行了广泛的实证研究，以评估 DreamerV3 在固定超参数下跨多个领域（超过 150 个任务）的通用性和可扩展性。我们设计了实验，将 DreamerV3 与文献中最佳方法进行比较，这些方法通常针对特定基准专门设计。此外，我们将 DreamerV3 应用于具有挑战性的视频游戏 Minecraft。附录 A 给出了各领域的概述。对于 DreamerV3，我们直接报告随机训练策略的性能，避免使用确定性策略进行单独的评估运行，从而简化了设置。所有 DreamerV3 智能体均在一个 Nvidia V100 GPU 上训练，使得该算法可广泛应用于研究实验室。源代码和数值结果可在项目网站上获取：https://danijar.com/dreamerv3

items.en: To evaluate the generality of DreamerV3, we perform an extensive empirical evaluation across 7 domains that include continuous and discrete actions, visual and low-dimensional inputs, dense and sparse rewards, different reward scales, 2D and 3D worlds, and procedural generation. Figure 1 summarizes the results, with training curves and score tables included in the appendix. DreamerV3 achieves strong performance on all domains and outperforms all previous algorithms on 4 of them, while also using fixed hyperparameters across all benchmarks.
items.zh: 为了评估 DreamerV3 的通用性，我们在 7 个领域进行了广泛的实证评估，这些领域包括连续和离散动作、视觉和低维输入、密集和稀疏奖励、不同的奖励尺度、2D 和 3D 世界以及程序化生成。图 1 总结了结果，训练曲线和得分表包含在附录中。DreamerV3 在所有领域均表现出色，并在其中 4 个领域优于所有先前算法，同时所有基准均使用固定超参数。

items.en: Proprio Control Suite This benchmark contains 18 continuous control tasks with low-dimensional inputs and a budget of 500K environment steps 40. The tasks range from classical control over locomotion to robot manipulation tasks. DreamerV3 sets a new state-of-the-art on this benchmark, outperforming D4PG 41, DMPO 42, and MPO 43.
items.zh: Proprio Control Suite 该基准包含 18 个连续控制任务，具有低维输入和 50 万环境步的预算 40。任务范围从经典控制到运动控制再到机器人操作任务。DreamerV3 在该基准上取得了新的最先进结果，优于 D4PG 41、DMPO 42 和 MPO 43。

items.en: Visual Control Suite This benchmark consists of 20 continuous control tasks where the agent receives only high-dimensional images as inputs and a budget of 1M environment steps 40, 27. DreamerV3 establishes a new state-of-the-art on this benchmark, outperforming DrQ-v2 44 and CURL 45 which additionally require data augmentations.
items.zh: Visual Control Suite 该基准包含 20 个连续控制任务，智能体仅接收高维图像作为输入，预算为 100 万环境步 40, 27。DreamerV3 在该基准上建立了新的最先进结果，优于额外需要数据增强的 DrQ-v2 44 和 CURL 45。

items.en: Atari 100k This benchmark includes 26 Atari games and a budget of only 400K environment steps, amounting to 100K steps after action repeat or 2 hours of real time 46. EfficientZero 47 holds the state-of-the-art on this benchmark by combining online tree search, prioritized replay, hyperparameter scheduling, and allowing early resets of the games; see Appendix T for an overview. Without this complexity, DreamerV3 outperforms the remaining previous methods such as the transformer-based IRIS 48, the model-free SPR 49, and SimPLe 46.
items.zh: Atari 100k 该基准包含 26 个 Atari 游戏，预算仅为 40 万环境步，相当于动作重复后的 10 万步或 2 小时实时时间 46。EfficientZero 47 通过结合在线树搜索、优先回放、超参数调度以及允许游戏早期重置，在该基准上保持最先进水平；详见附录 T。在没有这种复杂性的情况下，DreamerV3 优于其余先前方法，如基于 Transformer 的 IRIS 48、无模型 SPR 49 和 SimPLe 46。

items.en: Atari 200M This popular benchmark includes 55 Atari video games with simple graphics and a budget of 200M environment steps 50. We use the sticky action setting 51. DreamerV3 outperforms DreamerV2 with a median score of 302% compared to 219%, as well as the top model-free algorithms Rainbow 52 and IQN 53 that were specifically designed for the Atari benchmark.
items.zh: Atari 200M 该流行基准包含 55 个具有简单图形的 Atari 视频游戏，预算为 2 亿环境步 50。我们使用粘性动作设置 51。DreamerV3 优于 DreamerV2，中位数得分为 302% 对比 219%，以及专门为 Atari 基准设计的顶级无模型算法 Rainbow 52 和 IQN 53。

items.en: BSuite This benchmark includes 23 environments with a total of 468 configurations that are designed to test credit assignment, robustness to reward scale and stochasticity, memory, generalization, and exploration 54. DreamerV3 establishes a new state-of-the-art on this benchmark, outperforming Bootstrap DQN 55 as well as Muesli 56 with comparable amount of training. DreamerV3 improves over previous algorithms the most in the credit assignment category.
items.zh: BSuite 该基准包含 23 个环境，共 468 种配置，旨在测试信用分配、对奖励尺度和随机性的鲁棒性、记忆、泛化和探索 54。DreamerV3 在该基准上建立了新的最先进结果，优于 Bootstrap DQN 55 以及训练量相当的 Muesli 56。DreamerV3 在信用分配类别中比先前算法改进最大。

items.en: Crafter This procedurally generated survival environment with top-down graphics and discrete actions is designed to evaluate a broad range of agent abilities, including wide and deep exploration, long-term reasoning and credit assignment, and generalization 57. DreamerV3 sets a new state-of-the-art on this benchmark, outperforming PPO with the LSTM-SPCNN architecture 58, the object-centric OC-SA 58, DreamerV2 27, and Rainbow 52.
items.zh: Crafter 这个程序化生成的生存环境具有俯视图形和离散动作，旨在评估智能体的广泛能力，包括广泛和深度探索、长期推理和信用分配以及泛化 57。DreamerV3 在该基准上取得了新的最先进结果，优于采用 LSTM-SPCNN 架构的 PPO 58、以对象为中心的 OC-SA 58、DreamerV2 27 和 Rainbow 52。

items.en: DMLab This domain contains 3D environments that require spatial and temporal reasoning 59. On 8 challenging tasks, DreamerV3 matches and exceeds the final performance on the scalable IMPALA agent 60 in only 50M compared to 10B environment steps, amounting to a data-efficiency gain of over 13000%. We note that IMPALA was not designed for data-efficiency but serves as a valuable baseline for the performance achievable by scalable RL algorithms without data constraints.
items.zh: DMLab 该领域包含需要空间和时间推理的 3D 环境 59。在 8 个具有挑战性的任务中，DreamerV3 在仅 5000 万环境步下匹配并超越了可扩展的 IMPALA 智能体 60 在 100 亿环境步下的最终性能，数据效率提升超过 13000%。我们注意到，IMPALA 并非为数据效率而设计，但作为无数据约束下可扩展 RL 算法所能达到性能的有价值基线。

items.en: Solving challenging tasks out of the box not only requires an algorithm that succeeds without adjusting hyperparameters, but also the ability to leverage large models to solve hard tasks. To investigate the scaling properties of DreamerV3, we train 5 model sizes ranging from 8M to 200M parameters. As shown in Figure 6, we discover favorable scaling properties where increasing the model size directly translates to both higher final performance and data-efficiency. Increasing the number of gradient steps further reduces the number of interactions needed to learn successful behaviors. These insights serve as practical guidance for applying DreamerV3 to new tasks and demonstrate the robustness and scalability of the algorithm.
items.zh: 开箱即用地解决具有挑战性的任务不仅需要无需调整超参数的算法，还需要利用大型模型解决困难任务的能力。为了研究 DreamerV3 的缩放性质，我们训练了 5 种模型大小，参数从 800 万到 2 亿不等。如图 6 所示，我们发现了有利的缩放性质，其中增加模型大小直接转化为更高的最终性能和更高的数据效率。增加梯度步数进一步减少了学习成功行为所需的交互次数。这些见解为将 DreamerV3 应用于新任务提供了实用指导，并展示了该算法的鲁棒性和可扩展性。

items.en: Collecting diamonds in the open-world game Minecraft has been a long-standing challenge in artificial intelligence. Every episode in this game is set in a different procedurally generated 3D world, where the player needs to discover a sequence of 12 milestones with sparse rewards by foraging for resources and using them to craft tools. The environment is detailed in Appendix F. We following prior work 17 and increase the speed at which blocks break because a stochastic policy is unlikely to sample the same action often enough in a row to break blocks without regressing its progress by sampling a different action.
items.zh: 在开放世界游戏 Minecraft 中收集钻石一直是人工智能领域的长期挑战。该游戏中的每一集都设置在不同的程序化生成的 3D 世界中，玩家需要通过寻找资源并制作工具来发现一系列 12 个里程碑，奖励稀疏。环境详见附录 F。我们遵循先前工作 17，提高了方块破碎的速度，因为随机策略不太可能连续多次采样相同动作来破碎方块而不因采样不同动作而退步。

items.en: Because of the training time in this complex domain, tuning algorithms specifically for Minecraft would be difficult. Instead, we apply DreamerV3 out of the box with its default hyperparameters. As shown in Figure 1, DreamerV3 is the first algorithm to collect diamonds in Minecraft from scratch without using human data that was required by VPT 16. Across 40 seeds trained for 100M environment steps, DreamerV3 collects diamonds in 50 episode. It collects the first diamond after 29M steps and the frequency increases as training progresses. A total of 24 of the 40 seeds collect at least one diamond and the most successful agent collects diamonds in 6 episodes. The success rates for all 12 milestones are shown in Figure G.1.
items.zh: 由于在这个复杂领域中的训练时间，专门为 Minecraft 调整算法将很困难。相反，我们直接使用 DreamerV3 的默认超参数。如图 1 所示，DreamerV3 是第一个从零开始在 Minecraft 中收集钻石的算法，无需使用 VPT 16 所需的人类数据。在 40 个种子训练 1 亿环境步的情况下，DreamerV3 在 50 集内收集了钻石。它在 2900 万步后收集了第一颗钻石，并且频率随着训练进展而增加。40 个种子中有 24 个至少收集了一颗钻石，最成功的智能体在 6 集中收集了钻石。所有 12 个里程碑的成功率如图 G.1 所示。

items.en: Developing general-purpose algorithms has long been a goal of reinforcement learning research. PPO 19 is one of the most widely used algorithms and requires relatively little tuning but uses large amounts of experience due to its on-policy nature. SAC 38 is a popular choice for continuous control and leverages experience replay for higher data-efficiency, but in practice requires tuning, especially for its entropy scale, and struggles with high-dimensional inputs 61. MuZero 34 plans using a value prediction model and has achieved high performance at the cost of complex algorithmic components, such as MCTS with UCB exploration and prioritized replay. Gato 62 fits one large model to expert demonstrations of multiple tasks, but is only applicable to tasks where expert data is available. In comparison, we show that DreamerV3 masters a diverse range of environments trained with fixed hyperparameters and from scratch.
items.zh: 开发通用算法一直是强化学习研究的目标。PPO 19 是最广泛使用的算法之一，需要相对较少的调整，但由于其在线策略性质，使用了大量经验。SAC 38 是连续控制的流行选择，利用经验回放实现更高的数据效率，但在实践中需要调整，特别是其熵尺度，并且难以处理高维输入 61。MuZero 34 使用价值预测模型进行规划，并取得了高性能，但代价是复杂的算法组件，如带有 UCB 探索和优先回放的 MCTS。Gato 62 将一个大型模型拟合到多个任务的专家演示中，但仅适用于有专家数据的任务。相比之下，我们展示了 DreamerV3 在固定超参数下从零开始训练，掌握了多样化的环境。

items.en: Minecraft has been a focus of recent reinforcement learning research. With MALMO 63, Microsoft released a free version of the popular game for research purposes. MineRL 15 offers several competition environments, which we rely on as the basis for our experiments. MineDojo 64 provides a large catalog of tasks with sparse rewards and language descriptions. The yearly MineRL competition supports agents in exploring and learning meaningful skills through a diverse human dataset 15. VPT 16 trained an agent to play Minecraft through behavioral cloning of expert data collected by contractors and finetuning using reinforcement learning, resulting in a 2.5% success rate of diamonds using 720 V100 GPUs for 9 days. In comparison, DreamerV3 learns to collect diamonds in 17 GPU days from sparse rewards and without human data.
items.zh: Minecraft 一直是近期强化学习研究的焦点。微软通过 MALMO 63 发布了该流行游戏的免费研究版本。MineRL 15 提供了几个竞赛环境，我们将其作为实验的基础。MineDojo 64 提供了大量具有稀疏奖励和语言描述的任务目录。年度 MineRL 竞赛通过多样化的人类数据集支持智能体探索和学习有意义的技能 15。VPT 16 通过行为克隆从承包商收集的专家数据训练智能体玩 Minecraft，并使用强化学习进行微调，在 720 个 V100 GPU 上训练 9 天，钻石成功率为 2.5%。相比之下，DreamerV3 在 17 个 GPU 天内从稀疏奖励中学习收集钻石，无需人类数据。

items.en: This paper presents DreamerV3, a general and scalable reinforcement learning algorithm that masters a wide range of domains with fixed hyperparameters. To achieve this, we systematically address varying signal magnitudes and instabilities in all of its components. DreamerV3 succeeds across 7 benchmarks and establishes a new state-of-the-art on continuous control from states and images, on BSuite, and on Crafter. Moreover, DreamerV3 learns successfully in 3D environments that require spatial and temporal reasoning, outperforming IMPALA in DMLab tasks using 130 times fewer interactions and being the first algorithm to obtain diamonds in Minecraft end-to-end from sparse rewards. Finally, we demonstrate that the final performance and data-efficiency of DreamerV3 improve monotonically as a function of model size.
items.zh: 本文提出了 DreamerV3，一种通用且可扩展的强化学习算法，在固定超参数下掌握了广泛的领域。为了实现这一点，我们系统地解决了其所有组件中变化的信号幅度和不稳定性。DreamerV3 在 7 个基准上取得成功，并在基于状态和图像的连续控制、BSuite 和 Crafter 上建立了新的最先进水平。此外，DreamerV3 在需要空间和时间推理的 3D 环境中成功学习，在 DMLab 任务中以 130 倍更少的交互次数优于 IMPALA，并且是第一个从稀疏奖励端到端在 Minecraft 中获得钻石的算法。最后，我们证明了 DreamerV3 的最终性能和数据效率随模型大小单调提升。

items.en: Limitations of our work include that DreamerV3 only learns to sometimes collect diamonds in Minecraft within 100M environment steps, rather than during every episode. Despite some procedurally generated worlds being more difficult than others, human experts can typically collect diamonds in all scenarios. Moreover, we increase the speed at which blocks break to allow learning Minecraft with a stochastic policy, which could be addressed through inductive biases in prior work. To show how far the scaling properties of DreamerV3 extrapolate, future implementations at larger scale are necessary. In this work, we trained separate agents for all tasks. World models carry the potential for substantial transfer between tasks. Therefore, we see training larger models to solve multiple tasks across overlapping domains as a promising direction for future investigations.
items.zh: 我们工作的局限性包括 DreamerV3 仅在 1 亿环境步内有时学会在 Minecraft 中收集钻石，而非每集都能。尽管某些程序化生成的世界比其他世界更难，但人类专家通常能在所有场景中收集钻石。此外，我们提高了方块破碎的速度，以便使用随机策略学习 Minecraft，这可以通过先前工作中的归纳偏置来解决。为了展示 DreamerV3 缩放性质的外推程度，未来需要更大规模的实现。在这项工作中，我们为所有任务训练了独立的智能体。世界模型具有在任务之间进行实质性迁移的潜力。因此，我们认为训练更大的模型以解决跨重叠领域的多个任务是一个有前景的未来研究方向。

items.en: We thank Oleh Rybkin, Mohammad Norouzi, Abbas Abdolmaleki, John Schulman, and Adam Kosiorek for insightful discussions. We thank Bobak Shahriari for training curves of baselines for proprioceptive control, Denis Yarats for training curves for visual control, Surya Bhupatiraju for Muesli results on BSuite, and Hubert Soyer for providing training curves of the original IMPALA experiments. We thank Daniel Furrer, Andrew Chen, and Dakshesh Garambha for help with using Google Cloud infrastructure for running the Minecraft experiments.
items.zh: 我们感谢 Oleh Rybkin、Mohammad Norouzi、Abbas Abdolmaleki、John Schulman 和 Adam Kosiorek 的 insightful 讨论。我们感谢 Bobak Shahriari 提供本体感觉控制的基线训练曲线，Denis Yarats 提供视觉控制的训练曲线，Surya Bhupatiraju 提供 BSuite 上的 Muesli 结果，以及 Hubert Soyer 提供原始 IMPALA 实验的训练曲线。我们感谢 Daniel Furrer、Andrew Chen 和 Dakshesh Garambha 在使用 Google Cloud 基础设施运行 Minecraft 实验方面的帮助。
